_comment: Mechanism and policy are separated: the framework provides image text recognition, session reuse, retry, and assisted handoff mechanisms; this file decides which admissible source uses which mechanism. Product guardrails are still enforced centrally by the policy engine.
_guardrails: Low-risk, internal, and authorized sources may opt in to image text recognition after explicit configuration.
Official judicial, government-strong, and execution sources default to managed official-page confirmation.
If a policy attempts to enable image text recognition for guarded official sources, the engine records the request but routes to managed official-page confirmation instead.

challengeRiskModel._comment: Configurable scoring model for challenge admission. Lower scores are safer. The engine combines challenge type, source context, and authorization state before deciding whether automatic low-risk image text recognition is admissible.

internal-low-risk-demo.note: Example of a low-risk source-level opt-in.

judicial-zxgk-example.note: Example of a rejected high-risk OCR opt-in. The centralized guardrail still routes official execution/judicial sources to managed official-page confirmation.
